programmes.show_name: Cars: What's Driving Up Costs? Tonight
programmes.show_description: With the average cost of running a car now thought to be around £3,500 per year, Paul Brand investigates how rising costs are affecting motorists, and finds out if some risk being priced off the road altogether

Ainsley Harriott heads to the capital Nassau, where he climbs the Queen's Staircase walkway and visits the colonial-era Fort Charlotte. He joins Simeon Hall Jr to explore the deep roots of Bahamian history before rounding things off with a hearty island veggie stew pot and a warm Johnny cake
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programmes.show_name: Gary Barlow's Wine Tour: South Africa
programmes.show_description: The Take That star heads north with Jane McDonald as they touch down in Skukuza in the Kruger National Park. The singers enjoy a safari that includes a local breakfast favourite prepared by the resident head chef. Added to the mix is a local choir, high tea, South African sparkling wine and a magical experience with a family of elephants

Coverage of day three of the prestigious festival, where the feature races are the 3.20 Paddy Power Stayers' Hurdle and the 4.00 Ryanair Chase, and races also take place at 1.20, 2.00, 2.40 and 4.40. Analysis comes from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh, Mick Fitzgerald and Megan Nicholls, with reports by Matt Chapman, Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad and Luke Harvey, Social Stable updates from Oli Bell, betting news with Brian Gleeson, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programmes.show_name: Britain's Countryside Killer: True Crime Presents
programmes.show_description: Documentary examining the 2005 murder of 27-year-old Emma Caldwell, and her killer, Iain Packer, a sign-installer with a violent past, who evaded justice for years. It was one of Scotland's most high-profile unsolved murders until a TV documentary helped shed new light on the case
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programmes.show_name: Spring Clean: Keep, Chuck or Cash In? Tonight
programmes.show_description: Nearly half of the UK is living with stress-inducing clutter, but are the unwanted items lying around homes trash or treasure? The team investigates why many find it difficult to have a clear out, why having too much clutter can be bad for the health, and what viewers could upcycle or exchange for cash

Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar are joined in the kitchen by baker and writer Juliet Sear, who enjoys kimchi pancakes for breakfast and shares her tips for carrot cake. There is a lesson in sculpting clay chicken ornaments with crafter Jade Berry, as well as a sangria recipe with a twist

The competition is heating up with just 57 players left as Rob Brydon hosts another tense round of quick-fire quiz duels, with each win expanding a player's territory, while each loss sends another home. With a £5,000 Floor bonus on the line and a £50,000 jackpot up for grabs at the end of the series, it's all to play for as friends turn to foes and challenge one another
